question_text: A car travels along a straight, flat road at a constant speed of 20 m/s. The driving force from the engine is 5000 N. What can you conclude about the friction and air resistance forces acting on the car?

options.label: A
options.text: The friction and air resistance are less than 5000 N, so the car continues to accelerate

options.label: B
options.text: The friction and air resistance together equal 5000 N, so the resultant force is zero and the car maintains constant speed

options.label: C
options.text: The friction and air resistance together are greater than 5000 N, so the car is slowing down

options.label: D
options.text: There is no friction or air resistance because the car is moving at constant speed

explanation: An object moving at constant velocity has zero acceleration, which means the resultant force must be zero. Therefore, the driving force (5000 N forward) must be balanced by the total resistance forces (friction and air resistance combined) acting backward.
